hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a data-rich domain of additive-free lithium-ion battery SEM images and then fine-tuned on a data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then it will achieve significantly higher classification accuracy across NCM compositions and aging states compared to models trained without this two-stage transfer learning approach, because pretraining enables the network to learn generalizable morphological feature representations that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target-domain data.
